reviews.review_title: The best VR boxing game, avoid the 2nd
reviews.review_description: I am in no way an experienced boxer but I love to play fighting VR games and none of them have brought be back like this one, people complain about not being able to move but I don’t feel it’d be as good if you could, I play with pass through and a boxing ring sized play area and it genuinely feels like im boxing someone in my living room. The connections in this game feel clean and it helps with coordination, I know it doesn’t give actual boxing experience but it’s the closest thing you’ll get if you enjoy shadow boxing and want to have a fun intense workout. Avoid the 2nd one like the plague though, I played it and refunded it after 10 minutes, dont know how they destroyed such a good engine but the damage and connections in that game didn’t feel half as strong or good, I had it on hard difficulty and you can be put down in 2 punches but you have to hit 20 haymakers in a row perfect for them to even stagger, plus the models looked lifeless and were unresponsive to clean hits so you cant tell if you’re hitting them correctly, and the blocking and dodging was horrible, I would be punched through my blocks constantly and be hit by things I dodged. Dont even wanna mention the multiplayer, it’s horrible. On top of all of that charging 20-30 dollars on top of the base price for cosmetics is ridiculously scummy when your community has complained since release about the horrible damage and dodge system.

reviews.review_title: just one more update please bare fists
reviews.review_description: so everything about this game is awesome and i'm sure the second part is even better since you can play up against other players but if you could just add a bear knuckle fighting mode that would be awesome be able to cut your openent much faster and make them bleed much quicker and even if you turn up there health and durability it'll still knock them out fairly quick you know just do whatever you gotta do to make it realistically go by how it would be if two people bear knuckled brawled each other in the physics of the game lol

So this game is..alright I guess. If anyone says this is close to real, they're lying out their teeth. I'd put it close to that barfight game. The game says it calculates power, which is does in the training/practice area, but in the fight, if you swing too fast or hard, its like the game cant process it and some times you wont even hit. Beginning difficulties are definitely more realistic. 15 to 20 punches to knock the opponent down, higher difficulty you'll need to throw 50-100 punches. And they get up a lot.. A solid 3 star at best, maybe 4 but thats stretching it a bunch. 5 star is so unrealistic that id say they got paid off or they're fake reviews. Not saying dont try it, but have low standards and you'll like it.

I can land a combo on his face with 10 yellow punches and even a few reds and his face is left the same like he took 0 damage at all yet I get blinded even when im blocking his punches. The games easily levels got extremely hard for no reason

When I load the game im like 6 feet away from the thing i have to stand on so it can make a photo???

WARNING!!!! EXTREMELY addictive!!!! ⚠️🥊🔥

At a certain point it’s unfair to even call this a “game.” You don’t hop in to “play.” You hop in to TRAIN. Your brain treats it like an actual fight, and it sticks with you.

I have not used my gym membership in months. I am consistently sore. Not “ouch” sore, but “why do my shoulders exist” sore. The worst part is you will keep coming back because it is fun while it turns you into a cardio machine. Win or lose. You can’t stop fighting.

Two warnings:
	1.	Do not punch your TV. Seriously.
	2.	You will try falling asleep but will instead see flashes of being punched in multiplayer.

Buy this “game.”

And do not just take my word for it. Look at the rest of the reviews. Even the people who complain keep coming back!!!!

For a controller games idky it needs to see the gloves to know where they are🤦🏽‍♂️ ill throw the straightest jab and it would register as if i threw a overhand, getting ridiculous and second guessing buying the 2nd game

reviews.review_title: Nerfing player to promote new title
reviews.review_description:
Since the sequel was released I’ve noticed a significant change in gameplay. I’ll be drubbing my opponent all match then get knocked down after one or two of their hits land. The majority of the time now the first knockdown is a full ten count. I’ve also noticed my punches losing power throughout the fight. Haymakers that were landing before with orange or red glow now only get the grey. Very disappointed in the rug pull after a couple of years of enjoyment. Maybe only pull this stunt on people who haven’t purchased both games?

Update: Still sucks. It’s a good cardio workout, especially now that the frustration gets my heart rate up even quicker. Silver linings.

Update 2: Worse than ever. Tracking is way off lately just for this game. Throw a 5 punch combo and your gloves drift out of the ring for the last three. Lost so many rounds where red has 300K damage and blue has 9k damage that way.

It’s Me Goon yup the vr player im not going to make a review on this game all ima say is play it . Probably the only game where you can beat the duck outta someone and get better at fighting and a nice workout with realistic body damage

It’s not that I’m bad I know boxing and have been doing it for 3 years ￼alongside judo, taekwondo, mauy Thai, and Brazil Jitsu ￼so it’s not a skill thing it’s just not fun. It doesn’t feel like I’m boxing some one. It feels like I’m punching a wall￼. I hit them as hard as I can and they just eat it. And they hit me and it’s like a baseball bat. If you want a boxing game play a game like creed or  night club simulator they are actually fun. Don’t buy this game. ￼

This is a great cardio routine for anyone. I was a Boston street fighter and continued that type of workout with punching bags etc., but “ The Trill of the Fight “ game is the most awesome experience. I have had it for a short time so, I’m not sure what happens at its climax. The realism is mind boggling. Great game/cardio workout. It will help you lose weight and keep you fit. So, now now for the negative aspect of this game. You can only fight the main eight or so opponents. I hope Meta is planning an update with adding more opponents. I would also like to be able to pick a name/fighting name and an image instead of being referred to as “the player “.

This game is absolutely amazing if you want a box inside your own virtual reality experience buy it. It will be the best $12 you spent on. Have a good day. 
￼

I bought this game SINCE 2019. On the first Oculus Quest no less. The amount of praise it gets even to this day is a marvel and a serious wake up call for HalfBrick. Simple, yet detailed. Repetitive, yet more rewarding. This game paid attention to the overall average gamer physique and it shows. Custom matches to make fights easier on the body instead. Boxing forms are different from the last opponent you face with more variety and speed as the career mode progresses . It has been a exercising event and more people are returning to it. Not to mention, but the opponent does the one thing some, if not all, have said:

THE OPPONENT FOLLOWS THE DIFFICULTY AS MADE BY THE PLAYER!

The original will always be a cut above the sequel, and maybe you can find a boxer that pays attention to the more finer details.

From all the available boxing games, TOFT happens to be the best, most optimized and complete experience. I personally enjoy the Mixed Reality feature which should be a required mode for ALL boxing games. 

One feature that would be awesome: 
Add mitt work mode or give us a coach doing mitt work call outs.
I lost a ton of weight and had a lot of fun doing it.

I rate it five star. I can’t tell if it shows I rated with any stars. It’s the best. I work with this app along with Supernatural. Between the two programs you can stay in shape. I personally don’t know how anyone could say. This isn’t the best boxing app, assuming you have the equipment to properly use it. By that I mean a great headset and controllers so that you can actually box. For every hour I box I lose about 4 pounds until I get back into shape. Then every time I work out, it’s about 3 pounds you lose and if you can get through an hour of this well, that means that you’re in shape at any age. ￼

Love the game. It's fun while being a great workout. The only bad thing is that there are a limited number of opponents you can fight, but you can make adjustments in how hard or long the fight is and still keep it entertaining and challenging. Definitely stick to this game and don't buy the second one.

Incredible workout, excellent shadow boxing. You can train on a speed ball, heavy bag, or punching bag. Fighting with the opponents, each with 4 different levels of endurance and skills - excellent. If you are not at the top of your game, you will lose the fight eventually. If you want an even better workout, put some wrist weights and off you go. Great.

This is a must! This will help with gym sessions.
